patches.summary: Fixed correctness/security defects surfaced by glean review missions: AdaptiveLimiter.Wait pacing race, `items get` provenance always counting 0, ResolveByName json_extract field-name injection guard, maskToken short-token exposure, dead OAuth token-refresh path (Zotero is API-key auth), and concurrency-aware sync progress (\r garble under parallel workers).
patches.reason: glean static-audit/security-audit missions found real bugs in generated infrastructure files. Fixed in-repo per the local patch protocol. The Wait race, provenance count, and maskToken are SYSTEMIC (affect every printed CLI) and should be pushed upstream to cli-printing-press; the token-refresh removal is Zotero-specific (no OAuth endpoint).
patches.validated_outcome: go build/vet pass; new regression tests TestAdaptiveLimiterWaitConcurrentPacing (also under -race), TestCountResultItems, TestIsSafeJSONFieldName, TestMaskToken pass. Verified false positives left unchanged: SQL multi-statement read-only bypass (empirically: db.Query runs one statement and mode=ro blocks writes) and global cache invalidation (documented opaque-key design).

patches.summary: Added unit-test coverage for five previously-untested generated modules flagged by glean test-gaps findings: HTTP client (do/retry/cache/sanitize/cacheKey), sync (pagination/cursor-stuck/maxPages/single-object/access-warning + pure helpers), CLI helpers (error classification/filtering/formatting/pagination), search (extractSearchResults shapes + live-filter + local fallback), and doctor (interstitial detection/fail-on policy/cache report).
patches.reason: glean test-gaps missions (beads pg9a, rf41, bnt5, f6yb, hcaq) flagged P0/P1 untested logic. Tests use real httptest servers, real store.Open temp DBs, and temp dirs (no mocks) and exercise real branch behavior.
patches.validated_outcome: 32 new test functions; go build/vet and full `go test ./...` pass (fresh, -count=1).

patches.summary: Reworked incremental sync to Zotero's integer library-version model. Added a library_version column to sync_state with SaveLibraryVersion/GetLibraryVersion; surfaced the Last-Modified-Version response header via client.GetWithVersion (do() refactored to doRequest returning http.Header); syncResource now reads/sends an integer `since` and stores the checkpoint; `--since` changed from a duration string to an integer library version (matching `items list --since`); removed the dead parseSinceDuration/RFC3339 path.
patches.reason: glean bead aca7 (P1): sync converted --since and last_synced_at to RFC3339 timestamps and passed them to Zotero's `since` param, which expects an integer library version, so incremental sync silently never filtered. Required response-header plumbing the client did not previously expose.
patches.validated_outcome: TestSyncResourceVersionBasedIncremental (stores Last-Modified-Version, sends since=<stored> on the next run, explicit --since overrides) and TestGetWithVersion pass; go build/vet and full `go test ./...` pass (fresh).

patches.summary: P3 cleanups from glean: (e5o8) parallelize annotations export child-fetches via cliutil.FanoutRun instead of a sequential Get-per-item loop, preserving source order and fail-fast error semantics; (zog1) extract the duplicated HTTP-status detection ladder into cliutil.ClassifyHTTPError used by both the CLI classifyAPIError and the MCP error handler, keeping each layer's hint text and result type; (3ta0) add an overridable retryAfterNow clock seam so the RetryAfter HTTP-date/epoch tests assert exact durations instead of a loose 5-8s range.
patches.reason: glean beads e5o8 (perf), zog1 (maintainability: brittle strings.Contains(msg,"HTTP NNN") ladder copy-pasted across internal/cli and internal/mcp), 3ta0 (test precision on wall-clock-dependent parsing).
patches.validated_outcome: TestClassifyHTTPError + exact RetryAfter time tests pass; CLI/MCP error messages unchanged (only detection deduplicated); go build/vet and full `go test ./...` pass (fresh).

patches.summary: Reworked `tail` from a full-snapshot re-fetch into a deduplicated version-cursor change feed: per-resource cursor persisted in sync_state under tail:<resource>, ?since= delta fetch, upsert/delete NDJSON events, /deleted handling, per-cycle delivery to webhook/file sinks, and --follow=false single-poll support. Restricted tail resources to the four with a change feed (items, collections, searches, tags).
patches.reason: glean bead a91x (P1): tail re-emitted every object every poll with no dedup or version tracking, so consumers could not tell what actually changed.
patches.validated_outcome: TestEmitChanges_ChangeFeed and TestEmitChanges_WebhookDelivery pass: baseline emits upserts + records cursor; delta emits one upsert and one delete and advances the cursor; webhook sink receives the cycle NDJSON.

patches.summary: Sync annotations and PDF full text as first-class dependent resources: indexed parent_key/item_type/annotation_color/item_date columns on resources (populated from each item's nested data sub-object), idx_resources_parent_key/idx_resources_item_type, store helpers ItemsByType/AnnotationsForItem/Fulltext, an opt-in `sync --fulltext` pass (always sends since, fans out attachment fetches, stores fulltext rows FTS-indexed for search), and local-first reworks of items fulltext / annotations search / export / timeline with a --refresh live override.
patches.reason: glean bead hhup (P1): annotations/attachments were stored only as opaque JSON with no indexed parent/type columns and no full text, so every annotation/fulltext consumer fetched live (N+1).
patches.validated_outcome: store_hhup_test (indexed columns populated; ItemsByType + AnnotationsForItem join annotation->attachment->top item; Fulltext round-trip) and sync_hhup_test (syncFulltext stores ATT1, db.Search finds it, fulltext cursor advances) pass; full `go test ./...` green.

patches.summary: Made ZOTERO_API_KEY conditional in the remaining metadata (tools-manifest.json env_var_specs required=false + description, handleContext auth block required=false + description, README env-var table and `claude mcp add` note) and replaced the MCP command-mirror shell-out with in-process Cobra execution (cobratree.RegisterAll takes a *cobra.Command factory; inProcessHandler runs a fresh tree per call capturing OutOrStdout; deleted cli_path.go). Excluded the streaming `tail` command from the in-process mirror.
patches.reason: glean bead c4ke (P1): metadata hard-required an API key the local desktop transport never needs, and command-mirror tools shelled out to a companion zotero-pp-cli binary that fails when not on PATH.
patches.validated_outcome: cli_path.go removed (no SiblingCLIPath references remain); TestInProcessHandler_Success/_Error and RegisterAll smoke tests pass; go build ./... and full `go test ./...` green.

patches.summary: Added group-library support via per-library DB files: a --group <id> root flag (validated numeric) sets activeGroupID, which routes defaultDBPath to data-group-<id>.db and rewrites the API library prefix to /groups/<id> in newClient; a new `groups list` discovery command enumerates the user's groups; doctor reports the active library. Personal library is unchanged (data.db, /users/<id>).
patches.reason: glean bead 9bfn (P1): library scope lived entirely in config.BaseURL with no group discovery, selector, or storage isolation.
patches.validated_outcome: groups_test (rewriteLibraryPrefix for user/web/existing-group/no-segment cases; userIDFromBaseURL extraction + group-URL rejection; group-aware defaultDBPath; groups list JSON + table output; group base URL rejected) passes; full `go test ./...` green.

patches.summary: Added a Zotero-aware local query planner so --data-source local replays endpoint scoping instead of dumping all synced rows. New store query layer (internal/store/query.go): QueryItems applies itemType (indexed column), tag and collection membership (json_each), top-level filter, quick-search (FTS), sort/direction (json_extract), and limit/start; buildSearchDocument indexes a curated item document (key, bibliographic fields, creators, tags, annotation text) into resources_fts instead of the raw JSON blob; SearchByType scopes FTS to one resource type. resolveLocal routes item-list paths (/items, /items/top, /collections/{key}/items[/top]) through the planner and advertises meta.scoped; the search command's previously no-op default local branch now runs cross-resource FTS.
patches.reason: glean bead cvl6 (P1): the local store was a generic JSON cache with FTS bolted on — resolveLocal ignored endpoint filters/path scoping, the search command's default local branch was a placeholder that returned nothing, and FTS indexed raw JSON rather than Zotero search fields, so the headline offline mode could not match live reads.
patches.validated_outcome: query_test (itemType/tag/collection/top/limit/start/sort + quick-search + curated FTS doc) and fixture-backed parity tests (live httptest vs --data-source local return identical key sets and ordering for itemType+sort and tag scopes; empty scope returns [] not an error) pass; gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green. Scope note: implemented as a hand-written Zotero item read model (the high-value list/get/search scopes), not a fully generic per-endpoint translator.

patches.summary: Added first-class MCP resources and prompts so the server is no longer tool-only. Resources: zotero://context (durable domain taxonomy, shared with the context tool via domainContext), zotero://agent-context (CLI description via new cli.AgentContextJSON), zotero://status (local archive sync state), zotero://schema (local SQLite DDL), and templates zotero://collections/{key} (collection manifest) and zotero://items/{key} (item + annotations bundle). Prompts: inspect-library, export-reading-notes, prepare-citation-export. main.go advertises resource+prompt capabilities and calls RegisterResources/RegisterPrompts; mutations stay typed tools.
patches.reason: glean bead qfuq (P2): the MCP surface mirrored CLI commands only — no resource/prompt capabilities — so hosts had to shell through mirrored tools and guess command sequences to discover library state and workflows.
patches.validated_outcome: JSON-RPC tests through server.HandleMessage (as an MCP inspector would) confirm resources/list, resources/templates/list, and prompts/list return the new entries; resources/read zotero://context matches the context tool payload, zotero://agent-context carries schema_version+commands, zotero://status reports synced:false on an empty store; seeded-store tests confirm the collection manifest (item_count + member keys) and item bundle (annotation_count via attachment join) templates. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green.

patches.summary: Added `items enrich`, a provider-backed remediation pipeline that turns the read-only `items audit` work queues into fixes. --missing-doi resolves a DOI from CrossRef (exact normalized-title match to avoid wrong attaches), --missing-abstract fills the abstract from CrossRef by the item's DOI (JATS markup stripped), and --missing-pdf attaches an open-access PDF link from Unpaywall. It previews a patch plan by default and applies via the existing PATCH/POST paths only with --yes (or --agent), including the item version as a conflict guard and appending enrichment provenance to the item's Extra field. Provider base URLs are overridable for testing; apply is unit-testable via a small apiMutator interface.
patches.reason: glean bead dk33 (P2): the CLI had strong audit/reporting (items audit: missing_pdf/abstract/doi/tags) but no path from a detected defect to a safe, provider-backed fix — users had to export TODO lists into external scripts.
patches.validated_outcome: Tests: CrossRef DOI exact-title match (and rejection of non-matching candidates), CrossRef abstract JATS stripping, Unpaywall OA-PDF resolution (and no-OA case), apply step builds a PATCH body with version+field+Extra provenance and a POST linked_url attachment (via apiMutator), store-backed work queue produces a proposal for the matching item and skips the non-matching one, dry-run preview reports unapplied proposals, and an end-to-end apply through the real client to an httptest Zotero server PATCHes DOI+version. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green.

patches.summary: Metadata-enriched URL/DOI imports. `import url` previously created a bare webpage item whose title was the URL; it now runs a translator pipeline (internal/cli/import_translate.go): a DOI embedded in the URL resolves full metadata from CrossRef, otherwise the page's embedded citation_*/Open Graph/Dublin Core meta tags (the signals Zotero's Embedded Metadata translator reads) are mapped into a typed item (title, creators, DOI, date, publication venue, abstract, itemType), with a bare webpage item as the last-resort fallback. The CrossRef item mapper now also captures the abstract (JATS stripped), so `import doi` returns richer items. Both `import url` and `import doi` gained a --dry-run preview of the request body and preserve --collection. The CrossRef base URL is an overridable test seam.
patches.reason: glean bead 37jv (P2): import url/doi bypassed richer metadata and created sparse items (webpage with title == URL; DOI mapping limited to type/title/creators/year/DOI/publicationTitle), so one of the highest-frequency Zotero workflows produced references needing manual cleanup.
patches.validated_outcome: Tests: DOI extraction from URLs (article/PDF/none), HTML meta parsing (repeated citation_author) + typed item mapping (title/creators/DOI/journal/abstract/itemType), nil when no usable title, buildImportItemFromURL across DOI-in-URL (CrossRef, URL preserved, abstract), embedded-metadata, and non-HTML fallback paths, plus command-level --dry-run previews for url (collection preserved) and doi (abstract included) that write nothing. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green. Scope: provider (CrossRef) + embedded-metadata translator; the live Zotero desktop-connector translator and OpenAlex remain future sources behind the same pipeline.

patches.summary: Added `vault sync`, a vault-aware Obsidian/Logseq note materializer (internal/cli/vault_sync.go). One Markdown note per regular item, filename keyed on the native/BBT citation key (data.citationKey, falling back to extra then the item key), scoped from the local store via the cvl6 query planner (--collection/--tag/--item-type/--limit, top-level items only). Notes carry zotero:// backlinks (item select in frontmatter; per-annotation open-pdf with page label, group-aware via activeGroupID) and embed current annotations (hhup AnnotationsForItem join) in a fenced managed block. Re-runs are idempotent and non-destructive: only managed frontmatter keys and the fenced block change; user prose and other frontmatter keys are preserved. --dry-run previews created/updated/unchanged. Also wrote docs/obsidian-integration.md positioning the CLI vs the Obsidian/Zotero plugin ecosystem.
patches.reason: glean bead 49r4 (P2): note output was stdout-only template scaffolding (items note-template) with an annotations placeholder, and the only delivery sinks were stdout/file/webhook — no stateful, non-clobbering vault integration for the documented Obsidian/Logseq workflow.
patches.validated_outcome: Tests: Obsidian note creation (citekey filename, frontmatter, zotero://select + per-annotation open-pdf backlinks, page label, comment), idempotent re-run (unchanged, byte-identical), dry-run writes nothing, the critical no-clobber merge (managed keys updated while user frontmatter keys + prose survive), managed-block replacement without duplication, Logseq creation, filename sanitization, and --out required. Verified the native local API on Zotero 10.0-beta.7 returns full item data incl. data.citationKey. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green. Scope: Obsidian merge is the fully-featured path (user's tool); Logseq supports create + property/managed-block refresh.

patches.summary: Fixed --compact/--agent collapsing resource-envelope list items to {key}. compactListFields (internal/cli/helpers.go) applied a flat top-level allowlist, but Zotero items nest all real fields under data, so agent output for `items list` (and any envelope-shaped resource) reduced each item to {key}. compactListFields is now envelope-aware: when an item has a nested data object it keeps the top-level identifiers plus the data object compacted via compactObjectFields, and abstractNote/relations were added to that verbose-field blocklist.
patches.reason: Discovered while validating vault sync on Zotero 10.0-beta.7: `items list --agent` returned only item keys, useless to agents, because the generic compaction allowlist assumes flat objects and Zotero (like any JSON:API-style API) uses a {key, data:{...}} envelope.
patches.validated_outcome: TestCompactFieldsPreservesEnvelopeData asserts the envelope keeps key + nested title/itemType/DOI while stripping abstractNote/relations; the existing flat-object compaction test still passes; live `items list --agent` on Zotero 10-beta now returns the full useful item minus verbose fields. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green.
patches.upstream_issue: Systemic across printed CLIs whose API uses resource envelopes — candidate fix for cli-printing-press compactListFields (make it envelope-aware rather than flat-allowlist-only).

patches.summary: Added `schema drift`, a Zotero schema-currency probe (internal/cli/schema_drift.go) registered under the generated `schema` group. It fetches an order-independent fingerprint of the running Zotero's item types, item fields, and creator fields (and, under --deep, per-item-type field/creator-type validity), stores it as a baseline JSON file (~/.local/share/zotero-pp-cli/schema-baseline.json, not group-scoped because the schema is global to the install), and on later runs diffs the live schema against the baseline to report added/removed members. First run captures the baseline; --update re-baselines; --baseline overrides the path. Output is a pure function of the snapshots (no timestamps) so repeated runs are byte-identical. Added a `which` index entry for discoverability.
patches.reason: User asked how to detect what a Zotero beta upgrade adds; regeneration/awc7 do not discover anything (endpoints are frozen at Web API v3), but new item types/fields are the realistic between-version delta and the data is already available from the live API.
patches.validated_outcome: Unit tests: first-run baseline capture, no-drift on identical schema, added/removed deltas (+preprint -book, +citationKey), and human output, all against a real client (client.BaseURL) pointed at an httptest schema surface. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green. Live smoke on Zotero 10.0-beta.7: captured 40 item types / 123 item fields / 3 creator fields, no-drift on re-run, correct +item-types delta after a simulated upgrade, and --deep resolved per-type fields/creators for all 40 types. The probe strips the /users|groups/<id> library prefix because Zotero schema endpoints are global (/api/itemTypes).
patches.upstream_issue: Discovered pre-existing bug: the generated `schema item-types|item-fields|item-type-fields|item-type-creator-types|creator-fields` commands 404 against the local API because they prepend the /users/<id> library prefix to global schema endpoints. Systemic for any printed CLI whose spec mixes library-scoped and global endpoints under one prefixed base_url — candidate fix for cli-printing-press (per-endpoint base-path/scope override). schema drift works around it locally by stripping the prefix for its own fetches.

patches.summary: Added `items file <itemKey>` (internal/cli/items_file.go), registered under the generated items group. Resolves an attachment's on-disk location via the local-API file endpoint /items/<key>/file/view/url: treats the key as an attachment directly, else resolves the item's PDF attachment via /items/<key>/children (reusing findPDFAttachmentKey). Prints the file:// URL decoded to a filesystem path, or a JSON envelope {item_key, attachment_key, url, path}. Complements `items open` (which launches the desktop app) by handing an agent/script the actual file to read.
patches.reason: Coverage gap found while reviewing Zotero release notes/Local API docs: /items/<key>/file* (local-only attachment file access) was the one genuinely useful endpoint the CLI did not expose.
patches.validated_outcome: Unit tests (real client against httptest): direct attachment key, parent-item child-PDF resolution, and the no-attachment error. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green. Live smoke on Zotero 10.0-beta: `items file 9UXV5R7L` resolved its child PDF (RLRDANQN) to ~/Zotero/storage/RLRDANQN/...pdf (verified the file exists on disk); direct attachment key returns the same; percent-encoding is decoded.

patches.summary: schema drift now uses the Zotero-Schema-Version response header as a fast path: it probes /itemTypes once (capturing the header), and when the live version matches the stored baseline's version it reports no drift without fetching the remaining schema lists — a large saving under --deep (1 request vs ~80). Added client.GetWithHeader(path, params, header) to expose an arbitrary response header (mirrors GetWithVersion, cache-bypassing); schemaSnapshot gained a schema_version field; the no-drift/capture output and --json envelope now surface the version. Backward compatible: baselines without a stored version, or a Zotero that omits the header, fall back to the full fetch+diff.
patches.reason: Folding in a Local-API capability found while reviewing the docs: the Zotero-Schema-Version header is a single-request schema-change signal, avoiding the full multi-request fingerprint when nothing changed.
patches.validated_outcome: Unit tests: fast path issues only /itemTypes on an unchanged version (asserts itemFields/creatorFields are not fetched), and a version change triggers a full refetch + drift. Existing drift tests (no header) still exercise the full-fetch path. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green. Live smoke on Zotero 10.0-beta: capture reported '(Zotero-Schema-Version 42)'; re-run reported 'Zotero-Schema-Version 42 unchanged since baseline'.

patches.summary: Performance fixes from glean perf-audit beads. m4ku: items missing-pdf and collections stats queries now filter on the indexed item_type/parent_key columns instead of json_extract, using idx_resources_item_type/idx_resources_parent_key. x5lh: full-text sync persists the parallel per-item fetches in one keyed transaction via the new store.UpsertKeyed instead of one writeMu-serialized Upsert per item. cwtg: UpsertBatch reuses its already-unmarshaled object via extractIndexedColumnsFromObj instead of parsing each payload twice. rj6r/mhib: vault sync batch-loads annotations for all items via the new store.AnnotationsForItems (was one AnnotationsForItem call per item) and creates the vault dir once instead of per note. eedc: items enrich resolves CrossRef/Unpaywall lookups through cliutil.FanoutRun instead of sequentially. 2qhf: the items audit summary folds the abstract/DOI/tags counts into one conditional-aggregation scan (PDF count keeps its attachment anti-join).
patches.reason: glean perf-audit beads m4ku (P1), x5lh (P1), cwtg (P2), rj6r (P2), eedc (P2), 2qhf (P3), mhib (P3): hot paths bypassed indexed columns, issued N+1 DB/network calls, unmarshaled payloads twice, and ran multiple table scans. UpsertKeyed/AnnotationsForItems/extractIndexedColumnsFromObj are SYSTEMIC store helpers and candidates for upstream cli-printing-press.
patches.validated_outcome: New tests TestUpsertKeyed, TestAnnotationsForItems, TestQueryMissingPDFItems_IndexedColumns, TestQueryItemsAuditSummary_SingleScan pass; existing TestSyncFulltext_StoresAndIndexes, TestBuildEnrichProposals_DOIFromStore, and TestVaultSync* (incl. idempotent + dry-run) still pass, covering the changed call sites. gofmt clean; go build/vet and full `go test ./...` green (6 packages ok). Left open as generator residue / lower-ROI: vjbx (empty profiler-populated maps are upstream), z378 (would change shared store.List semantics), anjw (1-2 opens per command, not a hot loop), 6pv1 (the unmarshal backs a real emptiness filter).

patches.summary: Fixed the generated schema commands (item-types, item-fields, item-type-fields, item-type-creator-types, creator-fields, new-item-template) which 404'd against the local API because they prepended the /users/<id> library prefix to global schema endpoints. Added newSchemaClient(flags) in schema_drift.go (wraps flags.newClient + stripLibraryPrefix) and routed all six commands plus `schema drift` through it. Also skip write-through caching for the 'schema' resource type in data_source.go's writeThroughCache, because schema rows have no key and otherwise triggered a 'N/N schema items skipped (no extractable ID field found)' warning once the calls started succeeding.
patches.reason: Discovered while reviewing the Zotero Local API docs and verifying live: schema/type endpoints are global (/api/itemTypes), not under the library prefix the base URL carries, so every generated schema command had always 404'd against local Zotero.
patches.validated_outcome: TestSchemaCommandStripsLibraryPrefix drives newSchemaItemTypesCmd against an httptest server that serves only the global /itemTypes and 404s /users/0/itemTypes — the command succeeds only when the prefix is stripped. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green. Live on Zotero 10.0-beta: schema item-types/item-fields/item-type-fields/item-type-creator-types/creator-fields all return data with no warning. /items/new is global too but the local API does not implement it, so `schema new-item-template` still 404s locally (works only against the Web API).
patches.upstream_issue: Systemic for any printed CLI whose spec mixes library-scoped and global endpoints under one prefixed base_url — candidate fix for cli-printing-press: a per-endpoint base-path/scope override so global endpoints are generated without the library prefix.

patches.summary: Made local-API write rejections actionable. classifyAPIError (helpers.go) now detects the Zotero local API's verbatim write-rejection bodies via isLocalWriteRejection ('Endpoint does not support method' for POST/400, 'Method not implemented' for PUT|PATCH/501) and returns read-only guidance (point at the Web API + ZOTERO_API_KEY) instead of the misleading 'fix your auth' 400 hint. client.go excludes 501 from the 5xx retry loop so the rejection returns immediately instead of after 3 backoff attempts (~14s). doctor.go reports a new `writes:` field (unavailable for the local API; available/needs-key for the Web API).
patches.reason: User asked whether the repo can edit/enrich Zotero. The local API is GET-only (verified), so every mutation command failed with a cryptic, misleading error and a needless 501 retry storm; this surfaces the real cause and the path forward.
patches.validated_outcome: TestClassifyAPIErrorLocalWriteRejection (both rejection bodies -> read-only guidance) and TestClassifyAPIErrorAuthNotMisclassified (a genuine auth 400 is unchanged); TestPatchDoesNotRetry501 asserts a 501 yields exactly one request. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green. Live on Zotero 10.0-beta: `doctor` shows 'writes: unavailable (...)'; `items restore <key>` returns the read-only guidance in 0.038s (was a ~14s 501 retry storm).
patches.upstream_issue: Partly systemic: the 501-retry exclusion and a generic read-only-target hint are candidates for cli-printing-press (the local-API rejection strings are Zotero-specific and stay local).

patches.summary: Hybrid transport: when pointed at the read-only local API, mutating commands auto-route writes to the Zotero Web API while reads stay local. client.Client gains WriteBaseURL + a lazy ResolveWriteBase func + baseURLFor(method)/resolveWriteRoute (non-GET requests use the resolved web base, resolved once with a one-time stderr notice). newClient (root.go) wires the resolver when the base URL is local. write_routing.go adds resolveWebWriteBase (build https://api.zotero.org/users/<id> or /groups/<gid>) and fetchZoteroUserID (keys/current lookup against an overridable zoteroWebAPIBase). config.go gains a user_id field (+ ZOTERO_USER_ID env + SaveUserID) to cache the resolved ID. classifyAPIError maps HTTP 412 to a version-conflict/sync hint (reads local, writes cloud can race).
patches.reason: User wanted 'best of what's available': fast local reads plus working writes, without manually switching ZOTERO_BASE_URL. The local API is read-only, so writes must go to the Web API; a configured key is the consent signal.
patches.validated_outcome: TestWriteRoutingSplitsReadsAndWrites (GET->BaseURL, PATCH/POST->WriteBaseURL, resolver runs once), TestWriteRoutingFallsBackWhenResolverEmpty, TestResolveWebWriteBase (+ caching via keys/current against an httptest base), TestClassifyAPIErrorVersionConflict; gofmt/build/vet, full `go test ./...`, and -race on client+cli all green. Live on Zotero 10.0-beta with a real key: `items update <key> --dry-run` routed to https://api.zotero.org/users/99999/items/<key> with the notice (key masked in output), while `items list --dry-run` stayed on localhost; the resolved user_id cached to a throwaway config only.
patches.upstream_issue: The hybrid read-local/write-remote transport (lazy WriteBaseURL + resolver) is generic and a candidate for cli-printing-press; the Zotero specifics (keys/current, local-API detection) stay local.

patches.summary: Fixed `items create` body shape. The generated command POSTed {"items": [...]} (and an object from stdin), but Zotero's POST /items requires a bare JSON array of item objects, so every create failed with HTTP 400 'Uploaded data must be a JSON array'. Now the array from --items is sent directly, and --stdin accepts either an array or an object.
patches.reason: Found during a live write round-trip test: creating a throwaway item failed with 400 until the body was sent as a bare array.
patches.validated_outcome: TestItemsCreateSendsBareArray drives newItemsCreateCmd against an httptest server and asserts the request body unmarshals into a JSON array. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green. Live round-trip on Zotero 10.0-beta: after the fix `items create` created item XTTCA27C via the Web API (auto-routed), which propagated to the local API in <1s; cleanup delete (web) propagated to a local 404 in <1s.
patches.upstream_issue: Systemic: the generated create wraps array bodies as {"items": ...}; any printed CLI whose create endpoint expects a top-level array hits this. Candidate fix for cli-printing-press (emit a bare array body for array-typed request schemas).

patches.summary: Fixed `items delete` and `collections delete`, which 428'd ('If-Unmodified-Since-Version must be provided for delete requests') because c.Delete sent no version header. They now fetch the object's current version via GetWithVersion and send If-Unmodified-Since-Version via DeleteWithHeaders. Crucially they use a new rootFlags.newWriteClient() (root.go) whose BaseURL is the write target, so the version GET and the DELETE hit the same library (the Web API under hybrid routing) — correct even for an item just created on the web and not yet synced to the local mirror (an earlier local-version attempt 428'd in exactly that case).
patches.reason: Surfaced by a live write round-trip: deleting a throwaway item via the CLI failed with 428, and a first fix that read the version from the local mirror still failed because a just-created web item had not synced down yet.
patches.validated_outcome: TestItemsDeleteSendsVersionHeader and TestCollectionsDeleteSendsVersionHeader assert the DELETE carries If-Unmodified-Since-Version from the fetched version. gofmt/build/vet and full `go test ./...` green. Live full round-trip on Zotero 10.0-beta: `items create` then `items delete <key>` via the CLI returned 204 success (exit 0); the item is 404 on both the Web API and the local API afterward. Library left clean.
patches.upstream_issue: Systemic: the generated DELETE sends no precondition header; any printed CLI against an API that requires If-Unmodified-Since-Version (or similar optimistic-concurrency header) on delete hits this. Candidate fix for cli-printing-press (version-precondition handling on mutating verbs).

patches.summary: Addressed glean dead-code beads: removed orphaned generated packages internal/cache and internal/types; removed unused applyAuthFormat and the unused rootFlags.printJSON (hand-written commands now use printJSONFiltered); unexported test-only cliutil symbols (probeReachable, cleanText, parseStoredTime, rateLimitError); removed exported store.Open in favor of OpenWithContext; wired buildAgentDiscoveryContext from the curated which index.
patches.reason: glean beads i30e, 564e, 77k6, oqdg, co0m, os3f, u93a, 2kgy: orphaned packages, dead generated helpers, nil discovery placeholder, and test-only exported APIs added maintenance burden without production call sites.
patches.validated_outcome: Orphaned packages deleted; agent-context discovery now lists which-index candidate commands; all former store.Open test call sites use OpenWithContext(context.Background(), ...); gofmt/build/vet and full go test ./... pass (fresh, -count=1).

patches.summary: Commit 1 of glean 15e0 (vault write-back foundation): format/safety hardening for 'vault sync' with no Zotero writes. Distinguishes real read errors from missing files and writes notes atomically (temp file + rename, compare-before-replace) so a concurrent Obsidian/iCloud edit is never clobbered; rune-safe filename truncation; quotes managed YAML list scalars; adds zotero_key/zotero_library identity frontmatter and looks notes up by key (recognizing pre-key notes via the embedded zotero:// link) so a citekey change updates in place instead of duplicating; disambiguates filename collisions against foreign files; wraps the user '## Notes' region in stable begin/end markers (migrating a single legacy heading, reporting needs_notes_boundary when ambiguous); adds managed title/abstract fences on new notes so they refresh on re-sync.
patches.reason: glean 15e0 needs a safe, stable note format with durable per-item identity and a precise user-owned region before Obsidian->Zotero note write-back can land. Also corrects latent bugs: a swallowed os.ReadFile error could truncate a note, byte-boundary truncation could corrupt non-ASCII filenames, and unquoted collection scalars could break YAML frontmatter.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet clean; full go test ./... -count=1 passes (20 vault tests incl. byte-identical idempotency, stable lookup by key, collision disambiguation, legacy migration, needs_notes_boundary, atomic compare-before-replace, read-error handling, rune-safe filenames). Live smoke vs Zotero: a pre-commit-1 dev-vault note was recognized via its zotero:// link and migrated in place (gained zotero_key/zotero_library + notes markers, user prose preserved); re-sync reports unchanged (idempotent); fresh notes render the new fenced layout.

patches.summary: Commit 2 of glean 15e0 (still pull-only): adds a [vault] config section (root, notes_dir, format) so 'vault sync' resolves its output directory and format without --out on every run (explicit flags still win; ~ expanded); and adds a managed collection_names frontmatter key (collection-names for Logseq) resolving collection keys to display names from the local store, falling back to the key when a collection is not synced. Existing 'collections' (keys) is left intact for back-compat (Dataview).
patches.reason: glean 15e0 UX: opaque collection keys and a mandatory --out are friction for daily use. config.VaultConfig is read in vault sync; collection names come from store.List("collections", -1) so no store package change was needed.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet clean; full go test ./... -count=1 passes (config out-resolution + override, collection_names resolution, unit tests for vaultResolveOut/resolveCollectionNames). Live smoke vs Zotero: dev-vault note rendered collection_names: [Thesis] (real name for MAR7RFQN), re-sync unchanged (idempotent); a [vault] config with no --out wrote to root/notes_dir; missing config + no --out errors clearly.

patches.summary:
Commit 3 of glean 15e0: Obsidian -> Zotero note write-back. New 'vault push [--dry-run]' mirrors each note's user-owned '## Notes' region to one tool-owned Zotero child note (created via POST with a deterministic Zotero-Write-Token for retry-safety, updated via PATCH with If-Unmodified-Since-Version). Renderer is paragraph-verbatim (Markdown reproduced as readable

blocks, everything HTML-escaped, nothing interpreted) so wikilinks/tables/callouts are never mangled. Per-note sync baseline (note_key, note_version, source_hash, remote_hash, renderer) is stored in a hidden state comment, not in Obsidian Properties. Conflict detection compares the local region hash to the baseline and the remote note BODY (not just item version): a divergent remote is never overwritten — a conflict artifact is written under _vault-zotero-conflicts/ and reported. A locally-unchanged note whose remote body moved is reported remote_changed (honest status via a batched format=versions check). New 'vault conflicts' lists artifacts; 'vault resolve --keep-vault' republishes the vault copy using the live version (and --recreate re-creates a remotely-deleted note). Library-mismatched notes are skipped; group targets print a one-time warning. Reads stay local; writes go to the Web API via newWriteClient.
patches.reason: glean 15e0 deliverable: close the PKM loop so research notes written in Obsidian are published to Zotero safely. Push-only (no remote->vault merge) with conflict detection keeps it non-destructive; verbatim rendering and body-hash conflict detection avoid silent corruption and false conflicts.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet clean; go test ./... -count=1 and 'go test ./internal/cli -race' pass (verbatim renderer no-interpretation, state round-trip, region extraction, write-token determinism, atomic state write, out-dir resolution, push-note parsing). Live round-trip vs real Zotero on a throwaway item: dry-run wrote nothing; create produced a child note with verbatim HTML (markdown/wikilinks preserved); re-push unchanged; local edit -> PATCH update (verified on the web after sync lag); a real server 412 (stale baseline) -> conflict + artifact, 'vault conflicts' listed it, 'vault resolve --keep-vault' rewrote the note and removed the artifact; throwaway item deleted (204, cascades the child note).

patches.summary: Adds 'vault pull [--dry-run]' — the safe reverse of vault push. Brings remote Zotero child-note edits into the user-owned '## Notes' region, fast-forward only: a note is updated only when its local region is unchanged since the last sync. Both-sides-changed is reported as a conflict (artifact under _vault-zotero-conflicts/) and never merged. Only notes in the managed shape this CLI writes are pulled (marker-gated); the note HTML is converted back to text conservatively (reverses the verbatim renderer; external rich formatting degrades to plain text). Refactored shared helpers out of vault_push.go (replaceOrInsertStateComment, printVaultWriteReport) for reuse.
patches.reason: Completes the 15e0 round-trip so edits made in the Zotero app flow back to the vault, without a risky 3-way merge: fast-forward when safe, conflict otherwise.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet clean; go test ./... -count=1 and 'go test ./internal/cli -race' pass (push->pull round-trip is byte-lossless for content, managed-shape gating, tag stripping, region replacement, atomic region+state write). Live vs real Zotero on a throwaway item: push created a note; an external edit (items update --stdin with version) -> 'vault pull --dry-run' would-pull -> 'vault pull' rewrote the local region to the remote edit; re-pull unchanged; both-sides edit -> conflict + artifact listed by 'vault conflicts'; throwaway item deleted (204).

patches.summary: Refreshed the generated product docs (SKILL.md, README.md) to match the shipped CLI: corrected the now-misleading 'no API key required' claims to the real model (reads need no key; writes — items create/update/delete and vault push/pull/resolve — require a Web API key configured once via auth set-token and auto-routed to api.zotero.org, with a doctor 'writes:' line); and documented the previously-undocumented commands: the vault sync/push/pull/conflicts/resolve write-back workflow, the [vault] config block, and items file/fulltext/annotations and schema drift.
patches.reason: The product docs predated the hybrid write-routing and the entire vault feature, so they told users no key was ever needed and never mentioned vault. Generated-doc edits are cataloged per the local PATCH convention.
patches.validated_outcome: Both docs build no code (markdown); content cross-checked against the implementation — pull described as fast-forward-only/conflict-on-both-changed, push as conflict-safe with _vault-zotero-conflicts/ artifacts, auth as reads-local/writes-need-key. skill/SKILL.md is a symlink to SKILL.md so it tracks automatically.

patches.summary: Fixed 'items update' to send the version precondition Zotero requires for key-based writes. The generated command issued a bare PATCH /items/<key> with no If-Unmodified-Since-Version, so every write returned HTTP 428 ('Either If-Unmodified-Since-Version or object version property must be provided'). It now routes through newWriteClient and, unless the --stdin body already specifies a version, GETs the live version and sends If-Unmodified-Since-Version (mirroring the items delete fix).
patches.reason: Same systemic generator gap as the delete command: the press emits mutating commands without Zotero's version-precondition handling, so they 428 against the version-checked write API. Discovered when 'items update' was used to drive a vault-pull conflict test.
patches.upstream_issue: cli-printing-press: generated PATCH/DELETE/PUT commands should supply Zotero's version precondition (If-Unmodified-Since-Version) or they 428 on key-based writes
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet clean. Live vs real Zotero: 'items update <key> --title ...' on a throwaway item returned HTTP 204 (was 428 before the fix); item deleted (204).

patches.summary: Added a citation-readiness check to 'items audit' (glean dxut). 'items audit --missing-citation' lists citeable items missing a core citation field, annotating each with exactly which fields it lacks (creators, title, date, and the type-appropriate venue: publicationTitle for journalArticle/conferencePaper/preprint, publisher for book). The count is folded into the existing single-scan audit summary as missing_citation. Type checks use json_extract('$.data.itemType') rather than the indexed item_type column so the check is correct even on stores where that column was never backfilled.
patches.reason: glean dxut: surface items that cannot be cited cleanly before a manuscript handoff. Read-only and built on the existing audit infrastructure. Discovered while validating: the indexed item_type column is NULL for the whole local store, which silently zeroes the existing missing-pdf/missing-doi summary counts — the citation check deliberately avoids that dependency.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet clean; go test ./internal/cli passes (complete item excluded; journalArticle missing creators/date/publicationTitle and book missing publisher detected with exact field lists; summary count matches listing). Live vs real Zotero: 'items audit' summary shows missing-citation 97; 'items audit --missing-citation' lists items with per-item missing fields (e.g. 'date, publicationTitle').

patches.summary: Added 'items audit --verify-files' (glean dxut, on-disk half). For each PDF attachment that should have a local file (linkMode imported_file/linked_file/imported_url; excludes linked_url web bookmarks), it resolves the on-disk path via the local API (reusing fetchAttachmentFileURL + fileURLToPath from items file) and stat-s it, reporting attachments whose file is unresolved/missing/not-a-file. Honors --limit (one local-API lookup per attachment).
patches.reason: glean dxut: detect orphaned attachment records and broken storage (Zotero has the attachment but the file is gone) before relying on a PDF for citation/reading.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/-race clean; go test ./internal/cli passes (queryPDFAttachments selects imported_file/linked_file PDFs, excludes linked_url, non-pdf, and non-attachments). Live vs real Zotero: 'items audit --verify-files --limit 15' resolved and stat-ed 15 PDF attachments, 0 missing (healthy library).

patches.summary: Backfill the indexed resource columns (item_type, parent_key, annotation_color, item_date) for rows that predate them. The hhup migration's backfillColumns only ADDED these columns, leaving them NULL for every previously-synced row; the store's migrate() now also runs an UPDATE (json paths mirroring extractIndexedColumnsFromObj) guarded by 'WHERE item_type IS NULL', so it's a one-time idempotent no-op on populated stores.
patches.reason: NULL item_type silently broke every query that filters on the indexed column — on the live store 'items audit' reported missing-pdf 0 and missing-doi 0 (wrong) because 'item_type IN (...)' never matches NULL. Surfaced while building the citation audit (dxut).
patches.upstream_issue: cli-printing-press: a migration that adds indexed columns derived from the payload must also backfill values for existing rows, or queries on those columns silently return wrong results until every row is re-synced
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/-race clean; store test seeds NULL-column rows, reopens, and asserts they are populated from JSON (type-less rows become '' not NULL, 0 NULLs remain). Live on the real store: after one open, item_type populated for all 4067 items (0 NULL); 'items audit' now reports missing-pdf 103 and missing-doi 76 (were 0 before the fix).

patches.summary: Added `items summarize` (glean nbiv): a synthesis-ready context bundler, not an embedded model. For an item (or every item in a collection via --collection) it assembles the highest-signal LOCAL context — citation, abstract, the reader's annotations, a capped fulltext excerpt, and metadata gaps — plus a synthesis prompt, and lets the host LLM do the writing. Bounded and honest: --max-chars caps the fulltext excerpt, --max-annotations caps annotations, --no-fulltext omits it, and every cut is reported via `truncated` flags. --agent/--json emits the structured bundle; otherwise a readable Markdown brief. Collection mode batches annotations and fulltext to avoid an N+1 rescan. Also adds an MCP `synthesize` prompt that drives the auto-registered `items_summarize` tool.
patches.reason: glean nbiv (AI research synthesis): keep the CLI key-free and provider-agnostic by doing the deterministic assembly + token budgeting the agent is bad at, and delegating generation to whatever model the host already has. Mirrors the existing MCP prompt pattern (export-reading-notes).
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet clean; go test ./... -count=1 passes (pure buildItemBundle: citation/abstract/annotation mapping, annotation cap with kept/total, rune-safe fulltext truncation, gap detection incl. --no-fulltext, venue/citation/truncate helpers; MCP prompts/list includes synthesize). Live vs real Zotero: human bundle, --agent JSON (with truncated flags), and --collection mode all produced correct bundles + prompts. Fulltext excerpt path is unit-tested (no fulltext synced in the store to exercise live).

patches.summary: Added `import scan <dir>` (glean q1ia): a read-only, library-aware triage of a PDF folder. For each PDF it extracts a DOI (filename with slash-decoding, then the file's uncompressed embedded metadata via a head/tail byte scan — no PDF parser, no new dependency) and classifies it against the synced library: duplicate (DOI already an item), attach_candidate (matches an item missing its PDF), new (--resolve fetches a CrossRef title), or unidentified. Reuses normalizeDOI + crossRefItemFromDOI; --json for scripting.
patches.reason: Deliberately does NOT re-implement Zotero's 'Retrieve Metadata for PDF' (its recognizer + the Web API file-upload protocol the CLI lacks). The clear seam is the library-aware decision the GUI cannot make — which downloads are duplicates, which complete an item you already have, which are genuinely new — so the user decides what to import. Identifier->item resolution already exists (import doi/url/file).
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet clean; go test ./... passes (filename slash-decoded + embedded-content DOI extraction + none; classify duplicate/attach_candidate/new/unidentified with httpClient nil; DOI index build with case-insensitive keys + hasPDF). Live vs real Zotero: a 4-PDF folder classified correctly — duplicate (9UXV5R7L, has PDF), attach_candidate (10.1002/job.413 -> 5AQIZVY4, missing PDF), new (fake DOI), unidentified (no DOI); doi_source reported (content/none).

patches.summary: Added OpenAlex as a fallback provider to 'items enrich' (glean mmmd) for --missing-abstract and --missing-doi only. When CrossRef misses, it queries OpenAlex (/works?filter=doi: or title.search:), reconstructs the abstract from OpenAlex's inverted index, and records the provider in the proposal Source + Extra provenance. Key-free (optional --email / polite pool). --missing-pdf is left on Unpaywall (OpenAlex's OA data is just Unpaywall re-served). New --no-openalex disables the fallback; enrichOpenAlexBase is overridable for tests.
patches.reason: glean mmmd: CrossRef abstracts are spotty (publisher-deposited only); OpenAlex fills many CrossRef lacks, and resolves DOIs for non-CrossRef works. Deliberately NOT used for OA PDFs to avoid duplicating Unpaywall, whose data OpenAlex aggregates.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/-race clean; go test ./... passes (inverted-index reconstruction incl. repeated words; OpenAlex abstract + DOI resolvers via httptest; CrossRef->OpenAlex abstract fallback sets Source=OpenAlex and --no-openalex skips). Live vs real Zotero + real OpenAlex (preview, no writes): 'items enrich --missing-abstract --limit 120' filled 2 abstracts via OpenAlex (739 and 1023 chars) for DOI-bearing items CrossRef had no abstract for; DOI-less items skipped as expected.

patches.summary: Security/freshness bump of the generated go.mod/go.sum pins (glean dep-risk beads). modernc.org/sqlite v1.37.0->v1.52.0 (CVE-2025-3277 concat_ws heap overflow; also clears the '15-versions-behind' chore), which transitively raised golang.org/x/sys v0.31.0->v0.46.0 (CVE-2026-39824, Windows-only), modernc.org/libc v1.62.1->v1.72.3, modernc.org/memory v1.9.1->v1.11.0, ncruces/go-strftime v0.1.9->v1.0.0; pelletier/go-toml/v2 v2.2.4->v2.3.1 (fuzz-found datetime-unmarshal panics; our Config.TokenExpiry time.Time field sits in that path); mark3labs/mcp-go v0.47.0->v0.52.0 (session/transport hardening). golang.org/x/exp was dropped entirely by `go mod tidy` (no longer imported). No source changes; no inline // PATCH (a version pin is not a code site).
patches.reason: A glean dep-risk run (2026-06-18) filed a P0 CVE plus a cluster of dependency-staleness findings against the generator's pinned versions. Upstream candidate: CLI Printing Press pins these versions, so the durable fix is to advance the generator's go.mod template; bumped locally for the security fix. remyoudompheng/bigfft (P2 chore, no CVE) left at its Jan-2023 commit -- `@latest` resolves to exactly that (abandoned single-purpose lib pinned via modernc.org/mathutil), so nothing is actionable there.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt -l internal/ clean; go build ./...; go vet ./...; go test ./... -count=1 (6 pkgs ok, incl. internal/store on sqlite v1.52.0 and internal/mcp on mcp-go v0.52.0); -race on internal/store + internal/cli clean. Live vs real Zotero: `doctor` opened the 35MB data.db under sqlite v1.52.0; FTS5 `search` and `items audit --missing-pdf` (indexed columns) executed correctly; the MCP server (cmd/zotero-pp-mcp) JSON-RPC handshake under mcp-go v0.52.0 succeeded -- tools/list=76, prompts/list=[export-reading-notes, inspect-library, prepare-citation-export, synthesize].

patches.summary: Privacy scrub of real identifiers from tracked files (glean secret-sweep beads). Replaced the maintainer's real Zotero user id with the synthetic 99999 in the write-routing/vault-library code comment + unit tests, and replaced absolute home paths with ~/ or repo-relative forms in generator provenance (.printing-press.json spec_path -> spec.yaml, dogfood-results.json spec_path -> ~/...) and in two prior validated_outcome notes in this catalog. Source sites carry inline // PATCH(glean 61a2a8a9) markers; JSON metadata has no comment mechanism so this entry is the record.
patches.reason: glean bead 61a2a8a9 (real Zotero account id in patches + test fixtures) and 02dfb243 (OS username leaked via absolute home paths in tracked metadata). Privacy-hygiene only: not a credential, no rotation needed; the API key was always masked. History rewrite is intentionally out of scope (the values also sit in the import commit) -- scrubbed in the working tree per the bead's minimum recommendation. The 'enieuwy' printing-press handle is the printer identity, not an OS account, and is left as-is (outside both beads).
patches.validated_outcome: search for the real Zotero user id and absolute maintainer home paths returns no matches; legitimate synthetic paths (/Users/me, /Users/x in items_file tests) untouched. gofmt -l internal/ clean; go build/vet clean; go test ./... -count=1 (6 pkgs ok) incl. TestVaultLibraryID and TestResolveWebWriteBase under the synthetic 99999 id; all three scrubbed JSON files re-validated as parseable. No runtime impact: no Go code reads the provenance JSON (verified) and the changed source site is a doc comment + test fixtures.

patches.summary: Added `vault resolve --keep-remote` (glean inscribi-15e0 polish): the symmetric counterpart of --keep-vault. It pulls the live Zotero child-note body over the vault's `## Notes` region (discarding local edits), refreshes the per-note baseline, and clears the conflict artifact -- a forced `vault pull`. Extracted keepRemoteResolve(outDir,n,liveVer,liveHTML) reusing isManagedNoteHTML/htmlNoteToMarkdown/applyPulledRegion/removeConflictArtifacts. Resolve now requires exactly one direction (--keep-vault XOR --keep-remote; --recreate stays a keep-vault-side op) and the conflict artifact + divergence hints in vault_push.go/vault_pull.go now name both directions.
patches.reason: Closes a usability gap in the 15e0 bidirectional-sync feature: a conflict could only be resolved toward the vault (--keep-vault) or by hand-copying from the artifact. --keep-remote gives the discard-local direction a first-class command. Foreign (non-managed) remote HTML is refused so an arbitrary Zotero note is never imported into the managed region.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet clean; go test ./... -count=1 (6 pkgs ok) + -race on internal/cli clean. New unit tests: TestKeepRemoteResolve (remote overwrites region, baseline advances to live version, conflict artifact removed), TestKeepRemoteResolveRefusesForeignHTML (non-managed HTML refused, file untouched), TestVaultResolveDirectionValidation (keep-vault+keep-remote, keep-remote+recreate, and no-direction all rejected). Live vs real Zotero: created throwaway parent 3E8REQJF, `vault push` created child note 7QZ7HGHW, edited the local region, then `vault resolve smoketest --keep-remote` pulled the remote over it (local edit discarded, baseline refreshed); throwaway deleted (204). Auto-registered as MCP tool vault_resolve with the new flag.

patches.summary: Fixed non-product Glean security, correctness, dependency, and polish findings while leaving broad feature/product beads open.
patches.reason: The requested triage distinguished actionable defects from major product decisions. Local fixes cover dependency security bumps, URL/path escaping, private file permissions, response-size caps, Markdown/YAML/Logseq escaping, sync pagination/storage correctness, MCP prompt hardening, cache privacy, and misleading examples; broad workflow/architecture opportunities remain product decisions.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt applied to edited Go files; go test ./... passed (6 packages ok, 3 no tests); go build ./... passed; targeted regression tests for private URL fallback, start/limit sync pagination, and webhook delivery passed. Runtime preflight observed ./zotero-pp-cli doctor --json with auth configured, local API unreachable, stale cache; ./zotero-pp-cli agent-context --pretty succeeded; searches for the real Zotero user id returned no matches and remaining your-token-here use is the intentional auth env-var hint only.

patches.summary: Added a shared write-safety mutation platform: one preview/apply state machine + split plan/result JSON envelope + write gates, decoupled --agent from --yes, and built selection/tag/duplicate/collection/reading-list/saved-search/workflow write features plus context cancellation.
patches.reason: Triage showed the CLI was strong at read but had inconsistent, ungated mutations (items move applied without --yes and reported preview as failure; enrich mislabeled previews) and --agent silently auto-applied writes. This introduces one mutation helper (apply = --yes && !--dry-run; --dry-run wins; --max-changes default 500/50-under-agent; --allow-destructive only for truly-irreversible ops; --keys-from selection; fail-fast bulk) that every new write command reuses, with per-command MCP safety annotations.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt -l clean, go build ./..., go vet ./..., and go test ./... all pass (6 ok, 5 no-tests). Smoke: items move/tags add|remove|list, tags audit fix, items duplicates resolve, reading-list add|start|done, searches materialize, collections bundle, workflow run register and appear in agent-context + the MCP cobratree mirror; workflow run exits 1 on a failed step and 0 on success; --agent help no longer claims --yes. Synthesis was already provided by 'items summarize --collection' (no-LLM-by-design), so no redundant command was added.
patches.upstream_issue: Consider upstreaming the mutation state machine + gates to cli-printing-press so generated mutations preview-by-default.

patches.summary: Fixed six bugs found while demoing the CLI: sync ID extraction for name-keyed resources (tags), global-schema-endpoint 404s, resources composite-PK / ON CONFLICT mismatch, title-default duplicate over-merge, missing enrich --collection scope, garbage venue years, live search hitting the wrong endpoint, and local-mode list reads.
patches.reason: A live run surfaced that `sync` errored on 6/8 resources and several read/write commands misbehaved. Root causes: (1a) tags/schema resources are keyed by tag/itemType/field, not id/key, so extractID dropped them; (1b) schema endpoints are global, not under /users/0, so they 404'd; (1c) the resources table uses a composite (resource_type,id) PK but the code created id-only PK + ON CONFLICT(id), breaking upserts and risking cross-type id collisions; (2) duplicate resolve defaulted to title-matching (489 false-positive-prone merges); (3) enrich could not target a collection; (4) venue years used SUBSTR of freeform date; (5) live search queried /searches (saved searches) instead of /items?q=; (6) resolveRead treated local list reads as single-object lookups.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt clean, go build/go vet/go test ./... all pass (6 ok, 5 no-tests). Live against the open desktop: sync errored:0 with tags(841)/schema(40)/schema-item-fields(123)/schema-creator-fields(3)/searches(1)/items-trash(5) now stored; duplicates resolve default = DOI-only (2, was 489) and --title warns; items enrich --collection scopes; venues shows real years (Safety Science 1998-2018); search returns items not saved searches; collections list --data-source local returns rows. Composite-PK migration upgrades a legacy id-only-PK table in place (no-op on the already-composite live DB) and bumps StoreSchemaVersion to 2.
patches.upstream_issue: Several are generator regressions (id-only PK, /searches search endpoint, missing schema-prefix stripping in sync) worth fixing in cli-printing-press.

patches.summary: Fixed five user-reported field bugs in MCP search, sync transport/error reporting, SQLite read contention, and stale top-level alias archive rows.
patches.reason: Live MCP usage showed that boolean FTS queries like OR were silently literalized and empty MCP searches returned null, mirrored sync corrupted the stdio JSON-RPC transport by writing directly to process stdout and hid per-resource failure details, local read queries had no retry wrapper for SQLITE_BUSY_RECOVERY, and workflow status surfaced frozen items-top/collections-top residue from before alias canonicalization.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt clean; go test ./internal/store, go test ./..., go vet ./..., and go build ./... passed. Smoke: /tmp/zpp search "automation OR algorithm" --data-source local --json returned parseable live results; /tmp/zpp search definitely-not-present-field-bug --data-source local --json returned results:[] instead of null; /tmp/zpp workflow status --json and /tmp/zpp doctor --json no longer listed items-top/collections-top after migration; sqlite3 showed PRAGMA user_version=3 and no alias rows in resources or sync_state; a real /tmp/zpp-mcp JSON-RPC stdio session returned parseable stdout for mirrored sync, included the failed resource detail, and routed legacy sync_start/sync_summary chatter to stderr instead of corrupting stdout.

patches.summary: Added the flagship `library health` command: a read-only composite report that ranks findings from the existing audit primitives, with --for presets, a --fail-on CI gate (exit 11), and the precondition contract (live-only checks refuse loudly, exit 9).
patches.reason: Phase 1 of docs/roadmap.md. The CLI had strong individual audits (citekey-conflicts, duplicates, missing-* metadata, tag drift, verify-files) but no single 'is my library ready, and for what?' answer. `library health --for citation|systematic-review|quick|all` composes them into one ranked, finding-typed report; --fail-on gates CI; the live broken-attachment check declares a live_local_api precondition and emits a loud skip with remediation rather than silently degrading. Extracted shared SQL consts (citekeyAuditQuery, tagAuditDistinctQuery, tagAuditCountQuery) so the flagship and the primitives never drift. Added exit-code constructors gateErr(11) and preconditionErr(9).
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt clean; go build ./..., go vet ./..., go test ./... all pass (6 ok, 5 no-tests; new library_health_test.go covers check composition, presets, the gate, and the precondition skip). Smoke against the live 777-item library: `library health` (quick) ranks findings with a freshness header and loudly skips broken_attachment_file with remediation; `library health --for citation --fail-on high --agent` exits 11 with gate.status=failed; `library health --for quick --fail-on critical --agent` exits 9 with gate.status=indeterminate (broken-attachment precondition unmet).
patches.upstream_issue: Roadmap Phase 1 (docs/roadmap.md). Follow-up: the composed duplicate-title check inherits attachment-title noise (e.g. 165 items titled 'PDF') from the existing `items duplicates --by title` primitive; excluding attachment/note item types from duplicate-title detection would sharpen both.

patches.summary: queryDuplicateTitles now excludes attachment/annotation/note item types so attachments named 'PDF'/'Snapshot'/'Full Text PDF' are no longer reported as bibliographic duplicates.
patches.reason: The title-duplicate detector grouped by title+item_type but included non-citeable rows, so the live library reported ~20 attachment-title 'duplicate' groups (165 'PDF', 85 'Snapshot', ...). This polluted `items duplicates --by title`, the write-path `items duplicates resolve --title` (which would try to merge them), and the new `library health` duplicate_candidates check. Restricting to citeable item types sharpens all three at the source.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test pass. Live: `items duplicates --by title` dropped from ~29 noisy groups to 9 genuine bibliographic duplicates (real titles, no attachment names).

patches.summary: Phase 2 trust contracts: a reusable scope resolver (scope.go), a typed capability+preconditions registry (capability.go, surfaced via the `capabilities` command, agent-context, and the zotero://capabilities MCP resource), and `doctor --ensure-live [--launch]` (cross-platform launch + local-API readiness poll, exit 9 when unreachable).
patches.reason: Phase 2 of docs/roadmap.md. scope.Spec gives one cohort vocabulary (collection:/tag:/item:/query:/saved-search:/library) reused by `library health --scope`; saved-search declares a live_local_api precondition and refuses loudly. The capability registry is the single machine-readable source of truth for operation kind / write target / destructiveness / data sources / preconditions, so agents pre-flight requirements instead of guessing. doctor --ensure-live is the launch+readiness remediation that other commands' precondition messages point at; launchURI/launchCommand generalize the old macOS-only `items open` launcher across GOOS. The precondition vocabulary (live_local_api/web_api_key/synced_store/better_bibtex) is now shared across library health, scope, the registry, and ensure-live.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt clean; go build/vet/test ./... all pass (6 ok, 5 no-tests; new scope_test.go, ensure_live_test.go, and a scoped-health test). Live smoke: `capabilities` emits 106 entries with correct preconditions (searches run/items file -> live_local_api; library health -> synced_store; items citekey-conflicts -> synced_store+better_bibtex; items create -> web_api_key; items delete -> destructive+web_api_key; schema new-item-template -> web_api_key); agent-context embeds the same 106-entry registry; `library health --scope item:<key>` filters findings to that item; `library health --scope saved-search:ABC` exits 9 with a loud live_local_api precondition message; `doctor --ensure-live` reports reachable (exit 0) against the running desktop.
patches.upstream_issue: Roadmap Phase 2 (docs/roadmap.md). Generated files agent_context.go and doctor.go were PATCH-edited (field + command flags), not rewritten. Follow-up: --require-fresh freshness gate and query-planner read parity remain for a later Phase-2 slice; doctor.go is generated so the --ensure-live flags are a local PATCH worth upstreaming.

patches.summary: Added `library health --require-fresh <dur>`: refuses to certify the report (exit 12) when the local store is staler than the threshold, with a 'run sync + retry' remedy. Completes the Phase-2 trust-contract trio (scope + preconditions + freshness).
patches.reason: A stale local read should not silently pass a health gate, and CI needs to tell 'stale -> sync + retry' (retryable) apart from 'quality gate failed' (fix the library, exit 11) and 'precondition unmet -> launch/configure' (exit 9). This is the consumer that justified adopting exit code 12 (deferred in decision D3 until a real consumer existed). The report gains a `freshness` block; exit precedence is freshness (12) before gate (11). require-fresh is threaded via healthContext to avoid growing the assembleHealthReport signature.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... all pass (new TestLibraryHealthFreshnessGate covers stale/fresh/never-synced). Live: `library health --require-fresh 1h` on the 47d-old store exits 12 with freshness.stale=true and 'run sync and retry'; `--require-fresh 9999h` prints 'Freshness: OK' and exits 0.
patches.upstream_issue: Roadmap Phase 2 (docs/roadmap.md), exit code 12 now adopted. Follow-up: generalize --require-fresh to other local-read commands and to gate high-risk writes; query-planner read parity still deferred.

patches.summary: Added the first Phase-3 safe-remediation slice: `library health` now emits a preview-first `remediation_plan`, and `items enrich` accepts `--keys-from` so missing DOI/abstract/PDF remediation is exact-key scoped.
patches.reason: The roadmap explicitly rejected a new `library health fix` write engine: health diagnoses and existing commands treat. Before this patch, health findings could only recommend broad enrichment commands because `items enrich` lacked exact key input. `--keys-from` now filters the enrichment work queue before provider lookups, and health groups findings into remediation_plan steps that delegate to existing preview-first fixers (`items enrich --keys-from -`, `items duplicates resolve`, `tags audit fix`) without adding a new write path.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... all pass; new TestItemsEnrichMissingDOIKeysFrom proves provider calls are limited to the exact key list; library-health tests assert exact scoped remediation steps. Live: `library health --for all --limit 5 --json` emits remediation_plan steps for missing_doi/missing_abstract/missing_pdf with keys and `--keys-from -`, plus broad preview steps for DOI/title duplicate resolution and tag drift.
patches.upstream_issue: Roadmap Phase 3 (docs/roadmap.md). Follow-up: promote mutate.go into internal/mutation, add a run-journal, and add undo only for reversible actions.

patches.summary: Promoted the shared write-safety engine from internal/cli/mutate.go into a new flag- and transport-agnostic package internal/mutation (Options instead of *rootFlags; exported Op/Change/Envelope/Plan/Result; Run/CheckGates/Summarize/ResolveMode/Rows). internal/cli/mutate.go is now a thin adapter (flags->Options + envelope rendering).
patches.reason: Rule-of-three was met many times over: the mutation envelope had consumers across items enrich/move/tags/duplicates-resolve, reading-list, searches materialize, and tags audit fix. Extraction needed an Options struct to avoid a cli<->mutation import cycle (the engine read *rootFlags; rendering used cli terminal/JSON helpers). The cli adapter keeps runMutation/renderMutation/resolveMutationMode (legitimate flag+render layer, not shims); all callers migrated to mutation.* types via parallel fan-out. Envelope JSON wire format is byte-identical, so no behavior change.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... all green; engine tests moved to internal/mutation, adapter tests cover the flags->Options mapping. 7 caller areas migrated in parallel with no behavior change.

patches.summary: Added an append-only mutation run-journal and undo. Every applied run (>=1 change) is recorded as one JSON line in ~/.local/share/zotero-pp-cli/journal/journal.jsonl; `journal list`/`journal show <run-id>` inspect it; `journal undo <run-id>` reverses reversible (tag/collection membership) ops and loudly refuses the rest.
patches.reason: Roadmap Phase 3 audit-trail + undo. Recording is wired only on the real Execute() path and is best-effort (a journal failure never fails the mutation), so subcommand unit tests never touch the filesystem. Reversibility is decided per change field (tags/collections invert cleanly via Add<->Remove); DOI/abstract overwrites, tag renames, merges, and deletions are refused because the prior value was not captured or the inverse is unsafe. Undo is preview-first and routes through the same mutation engine + Web API write path; it is registered in the capability registry as a web_api_key write.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... all green. Package tests cover build-from-envelope, write/list/read roundtrip, and reversibility (refuses merge/DOI). Cli tests cover journal list/show, the apply-time recorder hook (preview does not record), undo preview plan (inverts tag_add, refuses missing_doi to stderr), and undo apply (PATCH removes the previously-added tag). Live: journal list/show/undo registered; capabilities marks `journal undo` op=write req=[web_api_key].

patches.summary: Added PMID (PubMed eSummary), arXiv (Atom), and ISBN (Open Library) import adapters as `import pmid|arxiv|isbn <id>`, mirroring `import doi` (--collection, --dry-run preview, bare-array POST /items).
patches.reason: Roadmap Phase 4 (bead 37849fc0): identifier coverage beyond DOI/URL/file. Each adapter has a package-level base-URL seam for testing and a pure response->Zotero-item mapper (pubmedItemFromSummary/arxivItemFromEntry/openLibraryItemFromData). Registered in the capability registry as web_api_key writes.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... green; unit tests on each mapper plus --dry-run JSON smoke per adapter. `import --help` lists pmid/arxiv/isbn.

patches.summary: Added `items new --item-type <t> --field k=v... [--stdin]`: fetches Zotero's /items/new blank template, validates provided fields against the schema (rejecting unknown fields), and POSTs. Reusable fetchItemTemplate/validateItemFields helpers.
patches.reason: Roadmap Phase 4 (bead 748370c5): schema-valid creation. /items/new is Web-API-only, so fetchItemTemplate returns a loud preconditionErr (exit 9) when offline, and forces the template GET live even under --dry-run (the read is a validation prerequisite; only the create is previewed). import apply reuses these helpers.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... green; tests cover unknown-field rejection and --dry-run merged fields. Orchestrator fix: /items/new returns a single object (not array) and the template GET must bypass dry-run.

patches.summary: Added Semantic Scholar (DOI+abstract fallback, behind --no-semantic-scholar) and OpenCitations (DOI-registration check) providers to `items enrich`, plus a read-only `--validate` discrepancy report comparing stored title/year against CrossRef and flagging DOIs absent from OpenCitations.
patches.reason: Roadmap Phase 4 (bead mmmd): higher-fidelity enrichment + validation. Semantic Scholar chains after OpenAlex for missing_doi/missing_abstract; --validate performs no writes and emits a JSON discrepancy report. useSemanticScholar threaded through buildEnrichProposals/resolveEnrichment alongside useOpenAlex.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... green; tests cover the Semantic Scholar fallback source and a --validate title discrepancy report.

patches.summary: Added the reviewable-import pipeline: `import resolve <dir|manifest>` turns a PDF scan into an editable JSON manifest (DOI-resolved items, per-entry action create/attach/skip), and `import apply <manifest> [--attach-mode none|linked-file|upload]` creates items and links files through the mutation engine (preview-first, journaled).
patches.reason: Roadmap Phase 4 (beads 7e799ea9 + q1ia): the write side of `import scan`. A shared manifest contract (internal/cli/import_manifest.go) decouples resolve from apply. resolve reuses the scan classifier + CrossRef; apply defers all I/O (schema-template fetch via items new's validateItemFields, item POST, linked-file attachment POST) into mutation Apply closures so previews need no network. attach-mode defaults to the safe `none`; `linked-file` creates linkMode=linked_file attachments; `upload` is refused loudly (multi-step stored-file protocol deferred). import apply is a web_api_key write in the capability registry; import resolve is read-only.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... green (7 pkgs ok). resolve tests cover dir->manifest and manifest refresh (httptest CrossRef); apply tests cover preview plan (create+attach with linked-file), default-none attach no-op, upload refusal, and the attachment-payload/created-key helpers. Live: import resolve/apply registered; `import apply --attach-mode upload` exits 1 with the loud refusal; capabilities marks import apply op=write req=[web_api_key].

patches.summary: Phase 5 trust plane: MCP resources zotero://freshness, zotero://health/{scope}, and bounded graph resources (collections/{key}/tree, items/{key}/children|attachments|context); a read-only `vault audit` preflight; and guided prompts prepare-library-health, prepare-import, sync-vault-safely.
patches.reason: Roadmap Phase 5 (beads 943783579 freshness, 04f41aa8 bounded graph, 725cb43f vault audit, a3d0987d guided workflows). CLI exports (FreshnessJSON, HealthJSON(scope), CollectionTreeJSON/ItemChildrenJSON/ItemAttachmentsJSON/ItemContextJSON) back the MCP resources so the mcp layer stays thin; all graph reads are depth/node-capped with a truncated flag. vault audit walks the vault read-only and reports orphaned/stale/upgradeable/needs_notes_boundary notes against the synced store. Prompts compose the shipped tools (library_health remediation, import scan->resolve->apply, vault audit->sync->push/pull) preview-first. zotero://capabilities was already shipped in Phase 2.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... green (7 pkgs ok); the mcp package test exercises resource/prompt registration. New CLI helpers + vault audit have HOME-isolated tests. Live: `vault audit` registered under vault; MCP registers freshness/health/graph resources and the three guided prompts without error.

patches.summary: Added `export snapshot [library|collection:KEY|tag:NAME] --output <file> [--resume]`: a truly paginated, resumable export that streams structured item JSONL and writes a <output>.lock.json lockfile (per-item key+version + a sort-invariant content sha256).
patches.reason: Roadmap Phase 6 (bead d27f99d4). The generated `export` does a single c.Get and never paginates; snapshot adds a header-free start/limit page loop with a checkpoint sidecar (interrupted runs resume via --resume) and a reproducibility lockfile built by reading back the complete data file (so resumed runs still fingerprint the full set). Uses structured item JSON, never the formatted-bibliography mode that ignores limit. Registered as a subcommand of the generated export parent via a one-line PATCH; read-only in the capability registry.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... green (7 pkgs ok). Pagination test fetches 250 items across 3 pages and resumes from a checkpoint; lockfile test proves sort-invariant hashing; command test exports 150 items across pages of 50, writes the lockfile (count 150, sorted, hashed), and removes the checkpoint on success. Live: `export snapshot` registered; missing --output exits 2; capabilities marks it op=read.

patches.summary: Phase 7 packaging & niceties: MCP installs honor env-selected library/profile (ZOTERO_GROUP/ZOTERO_PROFILE fall back into --group/--profile; MCP server logs the active library at startup); `groups inspect <id>` readiness preflight; `watch [resource...]` watch-mode incremental sync; `capabilities drift` endpoint-drift probe.
patches.reason: Roadmap Phase 7 (beads 3df91067 MCP profiles/groups/config, 9200a793 group readiness, 0cabee79 watch-mode sync, d130ba9e capability drift). The MCP runner executes through the root command's PersistentPreRunE (cobratree shellout root.ExecuteContext), so adding a ZOTERO_GROUP/ZOTERO_PROFILE env fallback there (explicit flags still win) makes every mirrored tool honor an MCP install's env config; base-url/config already come from env. watch reuses newSyncCmd per cycle (no edits to the generated sync.go) with an interval floor and SIGINT-graceful loop. groups inspect reuses the groups fetch to report read/write readiness. capabilities drift probes /items,/collections,/tags,/searches (limit=1) + global /itemTypes,/itemFields and reports unreachable endpoints.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... green (7 pkgs ok). Tests: env fallback applies ZOTERO_GROUP and an explicit --group beats it; groups inspect found/not-found JSON; watch interval-floor (exit 2) + one-shot cycle; capabilities drift checked=6 with /itemFields flagged drifted. Live: groups inspect/watch/capabilities drift registered; `watch --interval 1s` exits 2; MCP main logs the active library. Generated root.go and mcp main.go were PATCH-edited (env fallback + a startup notice), not rewritten.
patches.upstream_issue: Roadmap Phase 7 — completes the published roadmap (Phases 1-7 all SHIPPED). The ZOTERO_GROUP/ZOTERO_PROFILE env fallback and the MCP startup notice are local PATCHes on generated files worth upstreaming to cli-printing-press.

patches.summary: Fixed defects surfaced by a 5-reviewer pass over the Phase 1-7 arc: 4 P1 (export-snapshot --resume duplication + per-page flush; groups inspect over-claiming write; duplicate-resolve destructive gate bypass) and several P2/P3 (capability-registry items-trash mislabel and missing vault push/resolve writes; env-fallback explicit-empty flag; freshness age_seconds for never-synced; undo bulk-membership refusal; OpenCitations verdict; prompt backtick escaping; override-key guard test).
patches.reason: export snapshot opened the data file append-mode for any --resume even without a usable checkpoint (duplicating the snapshot) and never flushed pages before the engine advanced the checkpoint (an interrupt could leave the checkpoint ahead of the data) — now append only on a matching, not-done checkpoint and flush each page in onPage. groups inspect derived ready_for_write from the group's editing POLICY (near-always non-empty); now from the API key's per-group permission via /keys/current (new keyGroupWriteAccess), reporting key_write_known. items duplicates resolve trashed the duplicate with Destructive:false so --allow-destructive was never enforced despite the registry+flag-help declaring it destructive — set Destructive:true. InverseOps' merge-refusal rested on the {deleted} change being present; now InvertChange only inverts scalar (string) membership toggles, so a duplicate-merge's []string bulk add is refused regardless. capability registry: dropped the items-trash write mislabel (it is a GET list), added vault push/resolve as web_api writes, and added the asserting guard test the comment promised. env fallback now uses cmd.Flags().Changed so an explicit --group ''/--profile '' wins over env. freshness zeroes age_seconds for never-synced rows. OpenCitations validation now distinguishes a fetch failure (unverifiable) from a definitive not-registered result. promptArgumentLiteral strips backticks so a user value cannot break out of a Markdown code span.
patches.validated_outcome: gofmt/build/vet/test ./... green (7 pkgs ok). New/updated tests: groups inspect key-driven verdict (serves /keys/current), duplicate-resolve apply now requires --allow-destructive, reverse refuses []string bulk-membership undo, capabilityOverrides guard test asserts every key resolves to a runnable command.
patches.upstream_issue: Post-implementation review of the reprint-4.25.0 roadmap arc; the duplicate-resolve Destructive flag and the OpenCitations/groups-inspect accuracy fixes are candidates to upstream.
